rules.rule: A blank line must appear immediately before a return statement when other statements precede it in the same method body.
No line in a method may exceed 120 characters.
When a method call or declaration exceeds 120 characters, each parameter must be placed on its own line, aligned with the opening parenthesis.
Named parameters must be used when calling a method with literal arguments.
A method body must operate at a single level of abstraction.
Multiple consecutive single-argument method calls that form a logical stack must be written one per line with no blank lines between them.
LINQ or fluent chains that exceed 120 characters must be broken so each chained call starts on its own line, indented by one level.
A line break must not be introduced inside one chained call's argument list while other chained calls remain on the same line.
Method names must be full English words — no abbreviations.
